hero.title: Discover Your Professional Self
hero.subtitle: XIMA helps you understand your professional strengths and weaknesses through our unique 5-pillar assessment system, matching you with the perfect career opportunities.

ximatar_intro.storytelling: We don't define people with labels. We reveal the dynamic between what moves them, what lifts them, and what holds them back
ximatar_intro.explanation: In XIMA, a XIMAtar is not just a reflection of abilities. It is a living archetype — built from the tension between your highest potential, your current limitation, and your internal force of will
ximatar_intro.assignment_logic: Each XIMAtar is assigned based on: your strongest pillar (your edge), your weakest pillar (your friction point), and your Drive level (your inner fuel)
ximatar_intro.compass: A XIMAtar is not a label — it is a compass. It shows who you are, what shapes your progress, and what will elevate your next chapter

drive_paths.high_desc: Already activating change — moving with intention, taking initiative, and pushing boundaries
drive_paths.medium_desc: Stable but not leveraged — capable and consistent, with untapped potential waiting to be ignited
drive_paths.low_desc: Potential still dormant — not yet mobilized, but with latent energy that can be awakened

computational.name: Computational Power
computational.description: The ability to break down complex problems into logical steps, use data and tools to find efficient solutions, and think systematically about challenges
computational.as_strength: You are structured, precise, and fast under pressure
computational.as_weakness: You may struggle with systems, avoid complexity, or lose clarity

communication.name: Communication
communication.description: How effectively you express ideas, listen actively, build meaningful relationships, and collaborate with others to achieve shared goals
communication.as_weakness: You may hold back, lose clarity, or struggle to vocalize your thoughts

knowledge.name: Knowledge
knowledge.description: Your depth of expertise in key areas, ability to learn continuously, and skill in applying what you know to real-world situations
knowledge.as_strength: You synthesize deeply, connect insights, and ground decisions
knowledge.as_weakness: You may act on assumptions, avoid research, or stay on the surface

creativity.name: Creativity
creativity.description: Your capacity to generate fresh ideas, see problems from new angles, embrace innovative thinking, and discover unconventional solutions
creativity.as_strength: You see possibilities, adapt fluidly, and innovate
creativity.as_weakness: You may avoid risk, prefer the known, or resist ideation

drive.name: Drive
drive.description: Your inner motivation to pursue ambitious goals, bounce back from setbacks, take initiative, and maintain commitment to excellence
drive.drive_note: Drive doesn't define skill — it defines trajectory
drive.as_strength: You are self-motivated, resilient, and action-oriented
drive.as_weakness: You may hesitate, delay action, or lack momentum

computational_power.name: Computational Power
computational_power.tagline: Analyze, process, and solve complex problems with precision
computational_power.description: The ability to break down complex problems, analyze data, and find technical solutions through logical and structured thinking.
how_we_evaluate: Ability to automate processes and optimize workflows
Systems thinking and methodical approach to technical challenges
tips: Practice with logic puzzles and programming challenges
Develop data analysis and visualization skills

communication.name: Communication
communication.tagline: Connect, persuade, and inspire through words and actions
communication.description: The art of conveying ideas clearly, building meaningful relationships, and positively influencing through effective interactions.
how_we_evaluate: Expressive clarity and adaptation to different audiences
Active listening and conflict management ability
tips: Develop empathetic listening and constructive feedback
Study storytelling and non-verbal communication techniques

knowledge.name: Knowledge
knowledge.tagline: Understand deeply and apply wisdom with mastery
knowledge.description: The depth of understanding in specific areas and the ability to acquire, organize, and effectively apply information.
how_we_evaluate: Breadth and depth of specialized expertise
Continuous learning ability and knowledge transfer
Quality of documentation and knowledge sharing
tips: Keep a learning journal and structured notes
Teach others what you learn to consolidate knowledge

creativity.name: Creativity
creativity.tagline: Innovate, imagine, and transform ideas into reality
creativity.description: The capacity to generate original ideas, combine concepts in new ways, and find innovative solutions to existing problems.
how_we_evaluate: Originality in problem-solving and idea generation
Lateral thinking ability and unexpected connections
Balance between innovation and practicality in execution
tips: Explore completely new fields and disciplines
Experiment with prototypes and unconventional approaches

drive.name: Drive
drive.tagline: Pursue goals with determination and resilience
drive.description: The inner drive that keeps you focused, the ability to overcome obstacles and maintain momentum toward your goals.
how_we_evaluate: Ability to set and pursue long-term goals
Resilience in the face of challenges and setbacks
Initiative and proactivity in tackling new opportunities
tips: Set SMART goals with celebratable milestones
Develop routines and habits that support your goals
Find sources of inspiration and maintain clear vision

about.title: About XIMA
about.subtitle: Revolutionizing the job matching process through deep professional assessment
about.match_problem_subtitle: In today's job market, both employers and job seekers face significant challenges in finding the right fit.
about.match_problem_description: Traditional hiring processes often fail to identify the best matches between professionals and opportunities. Resumes and interviews provide limited insight into a candidate's true potential, while job descriptions rarely capture the real essence of a role or company culture.
about.solution_description: XIMA was developed to address this fundamental problem by creating a comprehensive assessment framework that looks beyond surface qualifications to the core attributes that determine professional success.
about.five_pillars_description: XIMA evaluates professionals across five fundamental dimensions that together provide a comprehensive view of their potential:
about.experience_pillar: Experience: Professional history, skills, and knowledge acquired
about.intelligence_pillar: Intelligence: Cognitive abilities, learning capacity, and problem-solving
about.motivation_pillar: Motivation: Drive, ambition, and what energizes the individual
about.attitude_pillar: Attitude: Approach to work, challenges, and collaboration
about.analysis_pillar: Analysis: Critical thinking, decision making, and reasoning
about.ximatar_description: Every professional's assessment results in a unique XIMATAR - a visual representation of their professional identity using animal characteristics that highlight their strengths and areas for development.
about.why_animals_description: Animal avatars provide intuitive, memorable representations of complex professional traits. Just as each animal species has evolved unique adaptations to thrive in its environment, each professional has developed distinct capabilities that make them valuable in specific contexts.
about.features_description: Each XIMATAR's appearance reflects the individual's assessment results, with physical features representing different strengths, capabilities, and potential growth areas.
about.mentor_matching_description: Based on the XIMATAR, XIMA pairs professionals with mentors who have complementary strengths, enabling targeted development in areas of opportunity.
about.job_seekers_2: Get matched with opportunities where you'll thrive
about.job_seekers_4: Connect with mentors who complement your profile
about.job_seekers_5: Increase job satisfaction and career progression
about.employers_1: Find candidates who truly fit your needs
about.employers_3: Build more effective and complementary teams
about.ready_subtitle: Join thousands of professionals who have found their perfect career match through XIMA.

q1.title: Understanding Your Approach
q1.body:
**Option A (Break into parts):** You prioritize systematic decomposition. Breaking complex datasets into logical segments (by region, customer type, time period) allows you to identify patterns more easily.

**Option B (Look for patterns):** You trust your intuition to spot trends quickly. This works well when you have domain expertise.

**Option C (Seek tools):** You leverage technology to handle complexity. Using the right tools can multiply your analytical power.

**Option D (Consult others):** You value collaborative problem-solving. Learning from others' experience accelerates your understanding.

**How to answer:** Choose what you ACTUALLY do first when facing new data. Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ua-CiDNNj30

q2.title: Understanding Your Approach
q2.body:
**Option A (Listen and clarify):** You prioritize understanding before contributing. Asking clarifying questions ensures alignment.

**Option B (Share and build):** You actively contribute ideas and enhance others' suggestions. Collaborative ideation energizes you.

**Option C (Take notes and summarize):** You help the team by capturing and synthesizing information. Documentation creates shared understanding.

**Option D (Focus on next steps):** You're action-oriented. Translating discussion into concrete tasks drives progress.

**How to answer:** Think about your last 3 team meetings. What role did you naturally play? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TP5xDjAMhQk

q3.title: Understanding Your Approach
q3.body:
**Option A (Hands-on practice):** You learn by doing. Building projects and experimenting cements your understanding faster than reading.

**Option B (Guides and documentation):** You prefer comprehensive understanding before application. Thorough reading builds solid foundations.

**Option C (Structured courses):** You value formal learning paths. Courses provide systematic coverage and validation.

**Option D (Mentors and colleagues):** You learn best from people. Human guidance provides context that documentation lacks.

**How to answer:** Think about the last skill you learned successfully. Which method did you use? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MlJdMr3O5J4

q4.title: Understanding Your Approach
q4.body:
**Option A (Unconventional solutions):** You naturally think outside the box. Generating multiple novel ideas before evaluating is your strength.

**Option B (Combine existing ideas):** You're skilled at synthesis. Remixing proven concepts creates innovative results with lower risk.

**Option C (Improve proven methods):** You build on what works. Incremental improvement is often more practical than reinvention.

**Option D (Cross-industry inspiration):** You look beyond your field for ideas. Fresh perspectives from other domains unlock breakthroughs.

**How to answer:** Recall a recent problem you solved creatively. Which approach did you use? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G8w0rJhztJ4

q5.title: Understanding Your Motivation
q5.body:
**Option A (Challenging goals):** Achievement energizes you. Hitting ambitious targets gives you deep satisfaction.

**Option B (Positive impact):** Purpose drives you. Knowing your work helps others sustains your motivation.

**Option C (Learning and growth):** Mastery motivates you. Developing new skills is intrinsically rewarding.

**Option D (Recognition and advancement):** External validation fuels you. Career progress and acknowledgment matter to you.

**How to answer:** All motivations are valid. Choose the one that MOST energizes you during difficult times. Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u6XAPnuFjJc

q6.title: Understanding Your Approach
q6.body:
**Option A (Measure metrics):** You rely on quantitative data. Specific performance numbers tell you if something works.

**Option B (A/B tests):** You believe in comparative analysis. Testing alternatives reveals the best option empirically.

**Option C (User feedback):** You prioritize qualitative insights. Understanding user experience matters more than raw numbers.

**Option D (Requirements verification):** You check against specifications. Compliance with requirements is your baseline for success.

**How to answer:** Think about a recent feature or solution you evaluated. Which method did you use first? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VQKMoT-6XSg

q7.title: Understanding Your Approach
q7.body:
**Option A (Find common ground):** You're a natural mediator. Identifying shared interests dissolves conflicts.

**Option B (Present facts):** You rely on evidence. Data and facts cut through emotional disagreements.

**Option C (Facilitate discussion):** You help others find solutions. Creating space for dialogue enables resolution.

**Option D (Propose compromises):** You're pragmatic. Finding middle ground moves things forward.

**How to answer:** Remember your last workplace disagreement. What was your instinctive approach? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CsgHMiOWsRA

q8.title: Understanding Your Approach
q8.body:
**Option A (Research more sources):** You triangulate information. Multiple sources reduce the risk of being misled.

**Option B (Analyze credibility):** You evaluate sources critically. Understanding where information comes from matters.

**Option C (Consult experts):** You defer to authority. Expert knowledge cuts through conflicting claims.

**Option D (Test empirically):** You believe in evidence. When possible, running your own experiment is the gold standard.

**How to answer:** Think about conflicting information you encountered recently. How did you resolve it? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6OLPL5p0fMg

q9.title: Understanding Your Approach
q9.body:
**Option A (Brainstorming sessions):** You thrive in creative group settings. Structured ideation generates quantity before quality.

**Option B (Study success cases):** You learn from what works elsewhere. Case studies provide proven templates.

**Option C (Prototypes and mockups):** You think by building. Tangible artifacts reveal possibilities faster than discussion.

**Option D (Diverse teams):** You value varied perspectives. Cross-functional input yields breakthrough ideas.

**How to answer:** When you need fresh ideas, which approach do you reach for first? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=K2vSQPh6MCE

q10.title: Understanding Your Motivation
q10.body:
**Option A (Set milestones):** You stay motivated through visible progress. Breaking projects into chunks with celebrations keeps energy high.

**Option B (Focus on final goal):** You're vision-driven. Keeping the end result in mind sustains you through difficulties.

**Option C (Vary activities):** You need novelty. Switching between tasks prevents burnout.

**Option D (Seek team support):** You're energized by collaboration. Feedback and encouragement from others keeps you going.

**How to answer:** Think about a long project you completed successfully. What kept you motivated? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mNeXuCYiE0U

q11.title: Understanding Your Approach
q11.body:
**Option A (Identify patterns, create scripts):** You're a builder. Creating custom automation solutions from scratch fits your technical mindset.

**Option B (Use and adapt tools):** You leverage existing solutions. Adapting proven tools is faster than building from scratch.

**Option C (Analyze cost-benefit):** You're strategic. Not all automation is worth the investment, and you evaluate before acting.

**Option D (Collaborate with experts):** You know when to ask for help. Technical experts can implement what you envision.

**How to answer:** Think about a repetitive task you automated (or wanted to). Which approach did you take? Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M576WGiDBdQ

q12.title: Understanding Your Approach
q12.body:
**Option A (Study the audience):** You research before communicating. Understanding background and needs shapes your message.

**Option B (Use relevant examples):** You connect through relatable content. Context-appropriate analogies bridge understanding gaps.

**Option C (Modify technical level):** You calibrate complexity. Matching detail level to audience expertise ensures comprehension.

**Option D (Ask for feedback):** You verify understanding. Checking comprehension prevents miscommunication.

q21.title: Understanding Your Approach
q21.body:

**Option B (Pilot projects):** You test before committing. Small-scale trials reveal real-world performance.

**Option C (Impact on processes):** You consider ripple effects. New technology affects existing workflows and teams.

**Option D (Case studies):** You learn from others. Industry examples show what works in practice.

open1.title: How to Answer Well
open1.body:
Strong answers describe a specific situation with measurable impact. Use the STAR method:

**Situation:** Set the context (what was the challenge?)
**Task:** Your specific responsibility
**Action:** What YOU did (be specific about your contribution)
**Result:** Outcomes with numbers if possible

Example: 'Led a team of 5 to reduce customer onboarding time from 2 weeks to 3 days, increasing activation by 40%.'

Be specific about YOUR contribution, not just the team's success. Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0vNUxleEyoo

open2.title: How to Answer Well
open2.body:
Self-awareness is key here. Strong answers identify a genuine growth area and show proactive steps to improve.

Avoid vague responses like 'I work too hard.' Instead, be specific:

'I struggle with delegating technical tasks. I'm addressing this by:
- Starting with small delegations
- Creating clear handoff documents
- Scheduling weekly check-ins instead of daily ones'

Show you're taking ownership of your development. Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tJxFZrMxT3s

open1.title: How to Answer Well
open1.body: Strong answers follow the STAR-L format: Situation, Task, Action, Result, Learning. Describe a specific decision with real stakes. Name the trade-offs explicitly (e.g., 'speed vs. thoroughness' or 'short-term revenue vs. long-term relationships'). Quantify outcomes when possible. Example: 'I chose to delay a product launch by 6 weeks to fix quality issues, trading short-term revenue for long-term brand trust. Result: 40% fewer support tickets and 15% higher NPS.' Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0vNUxleEyoo

open2.title: How to Answer Well
open2.body: Show self-awareness by naming a genuine growth area, not a humble-brag. Describe specific actions you're taking. Example: 'I tend to over-index on data and delay decisions. I'm addressing this by setting decision deadlines upfront, practicing 'reversible decisions' thinking, and asking my team to flag when I'm analysis-paralyzed.' Demonstrate you're actively working on it. Watch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tJxFZrMxT3s

guide.title: How XIMAtar Assessment Works
guide.subtitle: Discover your unique professional archetype through our comprehensive assessment system based on 5 core pillars.

step1.title: CV Upload
step1.description: Upload your CV for AI-powered analysis of your professional experience and skills.

step2.title: Baseline Assessment
step2.description: Select your field and complete the initial evaluation with multiple-choice questions.

step3.title: Deep Assessment
step3.description: Answer 21 questions plus open responses to reveal your unique profile.

step4.title: XIMAtar Assignment
step4.description: Receive your personalized XIMAtar archetype based on your pillar scores.

guide.pillars_description: Each pillar represents a core dimension of professional capability. Your unique combination determines your XIMAtar.

pillars.computational: Analytical thinking, problem-solving, and data-driven decision making.
pillars.communication: Clarity of expression, active listening, and collaborative dialogue.
pillars.knowledge: Domain expertise, continuous learning, and wisdom application.
pillars.creativity: Innovation, lateral thinking, and novel solution generation.

guide.scoring_description: Each pillar is scored from 0-10 based on your assessment responses. Here's an example profile:
guide.result_explanation: With highest Creativity (9.1) and strong Computational Power (8.2), this profile matches the Fox archetype - a Strategic Opportunist who excels at innovative problem-solving.
guide.ximatars_description: Each XIMAtar represents a unique combination of strengths and work style. Discover which archetype matches your profile.
guide.logic_step1_desc: Your responses to 21 multiple-choice questions and 2 open-ended questions are analyzed to calculate scores for each of the 5 pillars.
guide.logic_step2_desc: Your pillar profile is compared against the ideal vectors for each of the 12 XIMatars using distance algorithms and weighted matching.
guide.logic_step3_desc: A 25% matching bonus is applied when your strongest pillar aligns with a XIMAtar's primary characteristic, ensuring authentic matches.
guide.logic_step4_desc: The XIMAtar with the closest match to your unique profile becomes your archetype, along with personalized insights about your strengths and growth areas.
guide.cta_description: Start your assessment journey now and unlock personalized insights about your professional potential.
